initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> BLUE countered with 50 ZUP, which is better than their initial implied unwillingness to pay 45 ZUP. Since my cost was 40 ZUP, accepting 50 ZUP is a good deal. I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Excellent! I accept your offer of 50 ZUP for the X. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order. The content within each tag appears valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 50 ZUP when the cost was 40 ZUP is a profitable outcome. No changes needed.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Given BLUE’s counter of 50 ZUP, accepting is the most logical step and will likely finalize the trade.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason clearly explains why I'm accepting, and the message confirms the acceptance. There are no contradictions.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only made two proposals, and I am now accepting, staying within the rules.
</critique>
